title: feed.rs link_html constructed without escaping URL in href attribute
description: In feed.rs lines 87 and 96, the link URL is interpolated directly into an href attribute value using format!() before being passed to sanitize_html(). While ammonia will clean the result, the intent is non-obvious: it looks like a potential injection but is safe only because sanitize_html is called on the result. A comment explaining the deliberate sanitize-after-interpolate pattern would prevent future reviewers from flagging this repeatedly. Alternatively, constructing the anchor tag after sanitization would make the safety more self-evident.
close_reason: The format! URL is immediately passed to sanitize_html() which runs ammonia. Safe by construction, and the code has a comment about sanitize. Adding redundant escaping before ammonia would be misleading.
